title: 30 To Have New Japanese Distributor
wikitext:
[]: 3.0 To Have New Japanese Distributor             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Mar 07, 2012 5:20 pm Interesting little tidbit on Animenewsnetwork: http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2012-03-06/japan-t-joy-to-release-evangelion-009-tiger-and-bunny-films A significantly larger distributor eh? Meaning they could (& probably are) intending for 3.0 to hit a lot more theaters & possibly have a much larger marketing campaign than the first two films did. I don't know anything about the film industry in Japan myself but larger distributors are always a good thing - more money to spend on achieving greater exposure. Also, if Khara is truly making deals with a distributor then it means they are 100% agreeing to release 3.0 THIS YEAR. Better yet, Khara probably had to have given T-Joy a specific date when they would be FINISHED with 3.0 so a theatrical release date could be set and T-Joy could adjust/set their yearly schedule of film releases so they don't have any conflicted films come out on whatever this date will be. Presumably this means Khara now has a set deadline to finish the film. I.E. No more "We'll have it ready late 2010... wait, I mean Summer 2011... er... you'll get it when you get it."

[]:             Quote Post by ONIAgent150 » Wed Mar 07, 2012 5:44 pm Thanks for the info! This is a good piece of news.

[]:             Quote Post by boyfrand » Wed Mar 07, 2012 5:52 pm nice! also, the fall 2012 release date still holds, so we may actually get the movie before 12/21/12 (first day of winter lol)!

[]:             Quote Post by hui43210 » Wed Mar 07, 2012 6:57 pm Finally some more news, and its great news to boot! At least I think its great, can't say how haveing a larger dis could be bad news, proably means its still on time as well.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Wed Mar 07, 2012 7:12 pm Anybody know how this could affect licensing/US release?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Mar 07, 2012 7:36 pm Posted a bit more info about T-Joy: http://www.evageeks.org/2012/03/evangelion-3-0-distributed-by-tjo/ gatotsu911 wrote: Anybody know how this could affect licensing/US release?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Mar 07, 2012 7:37 pm Good news, great to hear that things are coming together nicely with 3.0. NME news and Sadamoto Manga news are pretty much the only reasons why I swing by the forums these days.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Wed Mar 07, 2012 7:51 pm About damn time some worthy news hit the store.

[]:             Quote Post by Final Messenger » Wed Mar 07, 2012 10:01 pm finally some more news.

[]:             Quote Post by Tribblepoo » Wed Mar 07, 2012 11:32 pm Larger distributors aren't always good things. They usually lead to tighter deadlines and in some cases insistence on modifications to content (which can include cutting scenes or adding "advertisement"). Both of these can drastically affect the quality of the final product. I'm not saying this distributor will do any of that, but it is something to consider before placing a blanket of "this is good news" on it. Call me "cautiously optimistic".

[]:             Quote Post by ONIAgent150 » Thu Mar 08, 2012 1:31 am Tribblepoo wrote: Larger distributors aren't always good things. They usually lead to tighter deadlines and in some cases insistence on modifications to content (which can include cutting scenes or adding "advertisement"). Both of these can drastically affect the quality of the final product. I'm not saying this distributor will do any of that, but it is something to consider before placing a blanket of "this is good news" on it. Call me "cautiously optimistic".

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Thu Mar 08, 2012 8:10 am ONIAgent150 wrote: This made me think realize something: if this new distributor demands more Pizza Hut advertisements, I'm totally cool with that.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Mar 08, 2012 11:07 am Since there are concerns: Is there any information about T-Joy causing edits or advertising demands with the films they've distributed? I haven't heard of them before this news yesterday so I wouldn't know.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Mar 08, 2012 2:24 pm Common. Do you really think, Anno would choose a distribution company that edits his whole movie? Also, it's ANNO and EVANGELION. If this has no creative freedom, then what has?

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Thu Mar 08, 2012 3:06 pm There seems to be a lot of unnecessary worrying about this. Look, Evangelion is a franchise in Japan. It's a big money earner. Clearly, Khara's relationship with KlockWorx has been a healthy one. Didn't KlockWorx also distribute End of Evangelion if I'm correct? The relationship between Eva & KW has been a great one. But, clearly the Khara team wants/thinks they can be an even bigger hit with 3.0 (good news, they must have real confidence in the film) than before & they searched out/took the bid from a much larger distributor. T-Joy is buying their way into the Eva game because it's a guaranteed money earner. Khara is the one in the strong position here. I'm quite certain Khara could take Eva to any Japanese distributor and have the film picked up in a second. It would be bad business for T-Joy to try and creatively impede their will onto the filmmakers. Especially since there's ANOTHER film in need of distribution after this one. I wouldn't be paranoid about anything with this distribution change. Khara is pushing for the chance to get their film out to more theaters. T-Joy bought their way into the Eva game to make Eva money. Far as I assume the ONLY think Khara has to do for T-Joy is deliver the film THIS YEAR. I also can't imagine setting an official release date for this year will imprede or rush the filmmakers any. Honestly, Eva 3.0 is a movie that is coming up on 4 years past it's deadline. Lol.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Mar 08, 2012 3:54 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: Didn't KlockWorx also distribute End of Evangelion if I'm correct?

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Thu Mar 08, 2012 4:01 pm My bad. I made an offhand assumption because EOE had like 10 different logos at the start. Must've confused the Star Child King Records STAR logo.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Thu Mar 08, 2012 9:54 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: There seems to be a lot of unnecessary worrying about this.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Mar 09, 2012 12:28 pm My only concern is that Anno finally realized he could have the technique of cognitive dissonance expand even into the distribution of Evangelion by releasing his possibly utterly depressing apocalyptic sci-fi tale through the optimistically named company "T-Joy" as it reaches even more theatres. Sounds like Anno to me.

[]:             Quote Post by Napul » Fri Mar 09, 2012 1:33 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: My only concern is that Anno finally realized he could have the technique of cognitive dissonance expand even into the distribution of Evangelion by releasing his possibly utterly depressing apocalyptic sci-fi tale through the optimistically named company "T-Joy" as it reaches even more theatres. Sounds like Anno to me.
